title: Delicate Light German Ham Braise
description: A yeasty and romantic German dish featuring ham in a delicate braise, suitable for an ayurvedic-sattvic friendly diet.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
Rinse the ham hock and pat it dry with paper towels.
In a large Dutch oven, saute the sliced onion, carrots, and celery in a little water until they are tender.
Add the ham hock to the pot and pour in the white wine and yeast broth.
Add the bay leaves and black pepper to the pot.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the ham for 2 hours, or until it is tender and easily shredded with a fork.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Shred the ham with two forks and stir it back into the braising liquid.
Serve the ham hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
